title: C# Booleans
content:
### Booleans in C#

A boolean data type represents one of two values: `true` or `false`. In C#, the `bool` keyword is used.

### Declaring Booleans

```csharp
bool isComplete = true;
bool isActive = false;
```

### Boolean Expressions

A boolean expression evaluates to either true or false:

```csharp
int x = 10, y = 20;
bool result = x < y;     // true
bool same = x == y;      // false
bool notEqual = x != y;  // true
```

### Using Booleans in Conditions

```csharp
if (isComplete)
{
    Console.WriteLine("Task is complete");
}
else
{
    Console.WriteLine("Task is not complete");
}
```

### Logical Operators with Booleans

| Expression | Result |
|------------|--------|
| `true && true` | true |
| `true && false` | false |
| `false && true` | false |
| `false && false` | false |
| `true || true` | true |
| `true || false` | true |
| `false || true` | true |
| `false || false` | false |
| `!true` | false |
| `!false` | true |

### Boolean Methods

The `bool` type provides useful methods:

```csharp
bool.TryParse("true", out bool parsed);  // parsed = true
bool result = bool.Parse("false");         // result = false
string text = true.ToString();             // "True"
```

### Comparison Operators Return Bool

```csharp
int a = 5, b = 10;
Console.WriteLine(a > b);    // False
Console.WriteLine(a < b);    // True
Console.WriteLine(a == b);   // False
Console.WriteLine(a != b);   // True
```

> **Key Point:** In C#, booleans cannot be implicitly converted to integers (0/1). You must explicitly compare. Use `&&` and `||` for short-circuit evaluation. A boolean expression is any expression that evaluates to `true` or `false`.
